seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with BeeAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with BeeAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: BeeAI enables robust workflow automation agents that orchestrate multi-step tasks with API integrations, conditional logic, and human approvals. Vex provides essential guardrails to prevent memory leaks, tool injection attacks, serialization failures, and model output inconsistencies common in production BeeAI deployments. This integration guide shows how to register Vex as an event listener to ensure reliable, secure workflow execution.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with BeeAI
architectureOverview.description:
BeeAI workflow agents follow a structured multi-step execution pattern: task decomposition → API orchestration → conditional branching → human approval gates → final execution. Each step maintains agent memory for context, uses tools for external API calls, and persists state across sessions. The architecture supports rollback mechanisms and idempotent operations for resilience.

Key components include: BeeAI Agent with Granite LLM, ToolRegistry for API integrations, MemoryManager for session state, and EventBus for observability. Human-in-the-loop occurs via approval callbacks before high-risk actions. Vex integrates via the EventBus to monitor and validate every execution phase.

This pattern excels for enterprise workflows like order processing, invoice approval, and customer onboarding, but requires strict guardrails for production reliability.

risks.name: Unbounded Agent Memory Growth
risks.description: Long-running workflow sessions accumulate conversation history without pruning, causing OOM errors and degraded performance.
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors memory usage per session and enforces automatic truncation thresholds.

risks.name: Tool Injection via Prompt Manipulation
risks.description: Adversarial inputs bypass tool validation, enabling unauthorized API calls or data exfiltration.
risks.mitigation: Vex validates tool parameters against predefined schemas before execution.

risks.name: State Serialization Failures
risks.description: Agent memory contains non-serializable objects, breaking persistence across restarts or human handoffs.
risks.mitigation: Vex sanitizes and validates state before persistence with automatic recovery.

risks.name: Granite Output Format Drift
risks.description: Model outputs deviate from expected JSON/tool call formats, breaking workflow parsing.
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces structured output validation with fallback prompting.

implementation.title: Basic Workflow Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize BeeAI agent with workflow tools and memory for multi-step task execution.
implementation.code:
from beeai import Agent, MemoryManager
from beeai.tools import ToolRegistry

# Initialize workflow agent
memory = MemoryManager(max_history=50)
tool_registry = ToolRegistry()

agent = Agent(
    model='granite-13b',
    memory=memory,
    tools=tool_registry,
    system_prompt="You are a workflow automation agent. Execute tasks step-by-step with API calls and request human approval for high-risk actions."
)

# Register workflow tools
tool_registry.register_tool('api_call', {
    'description': 'Make authenticated API calls',
    'parameters': {'url': 'str', 'method': 'str', 'payload': 'dict'}
})

implementation.title: Multi-Step Task Decomposition
implementation.description: Agent breaks down complex workflows into sequential, conditional steps with state tracking.

implementation.title: Idempotent API Integration
implementation.description: Ensure API calls are safe to retry with built-in idempotency keys and rollback support.

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval
implementation.description: Implement approval gates for high-risk actions with state preservation.
implementation.code:
def request_human_approval(summary: str) -> bool:
    # Persist current state before approval
    agent.memory.checkpoint()
    
    print(f"\n🔍 APPROVAL REQUIRED:")
    print(f"Summary: {summary}")
    print(f"Current State: {agent.memory.get_summary()}")
    
    response = input("Approve? (y/n): ").lower()
    return response == 'y'

# Integrated into workflow
if step.risk_level == 'high':
    if not request_human_approval(step.description):
        agent.restore_checkpoint()
        return "Action blocked by human review"

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Event Listener
vexIntegration.description: Hook Vex into BeeAI's EventBus to monitor all agent events with workflow-specific verifications.

vexIntegration.title: Memory Growth Guardrails
vexIntegration.description: Prevent unbounded memory accumulation in long-running workflow sessions.
vexIntegration.code:
@vex.guard('memory_overflow')
def monitor_workflow_memory(event):
    session_memory = len(event.agent_memory)
    
    if session_memory > 100:  # Max 100 exchanges
        # Auto-prune oldest exchanges
        event.agent_memory = event.agent_memory[-75:]
        vex.log.warning(f"Memory pruned from {session_memory} to 75 exchanges")
    
    # Enforce memory budget
    if event.memory_size > 50_000_000:  # 50MB limit
        raise MemoryOverflowError("Workflow memory limit exceeded")

bus.register_listener(monitor_workflow_memory)

vexIntegration.title: Tool Injection + Output Validation
vexIntegration.description: Block malicious tool calls and enforce Granite output formatting.

cta.heading: Secure Your BeeAI Workflow Automation
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and register as BeeAI event listener to eliminate workflow failures.
